scenario_name: P5 — Chinese/English code-switch SaaS dashboard user
stack: A6 lib synth + Triage v3 + Playback v2 (Meta v1)
verdict_rationale: Session terminated at T5 with covered_slots=[] (4/7 slots peak, then silent-zero, then user_input_unparseable_no_spec). No spec.md produced. This is a regression vs M4 (Triage-v2 alone) which reached 7/7 + done + conf=1.0 on the same answer set. The proximate root cause is NOT the triage v3 classifier itself: T2/T3/T4 retries all classified as relevant and credited the correct slot. The root cause is a transcript-persistence dedup bug exposed when SiliconFlow flaked TWICE in this run (vs once in M4). Sequence: T4 first 500 -> T4 retry 200 (credited first_run). T5 first attempt then ran with a corrupted prompt: the T5-main capsule shows assistant-T4-mirror and user-T4-answer DUPLICATED back-to-back ([4]/[5] then [6]/[7]) before the trailing 'Produce your turn-5 output' contract line. The Meta LLM, seeing a repeated user turn, emitted a malformed/empty envelope -> backend F6 silent-zero (covered=[], open=[], conf=0.0) in 775ms. T5 retry then ran fresh against question 'sprint速度图具体应该展示什么数据?' (a first_run drill-down, NOT robustness) — but our scripted A5 answered robustness, so triage-v3 correctly flagged it as off-topic and the kernel terminated via the user_input_unparseable_no_spec gate (which now uses Playback v2 + double-fail short-circuit instead of M4's predicate_done_no_spec_pending_synthesis path). A6 lib synth never had a chance to run because terminate fired before done=true.

metrics.min_latency_note: T5 first attempt at 775ms is the F6 silent-zero short-circuit, NOT a normal Meta-LLM round-trip

spec_md_check.note: spec.md was not produced because session terminated before done=true. A6 lib synth never ran. Entity preservation can only be evaluated against in-flight question_text — see questions_entity_check below.

questions_entity_check.note: Of the entities Meta did echo, all 5 are real user-mentioned entities. Zero hallucinated entities observed. The 4 'not yet echoed' entities (k8s/Okta/SSO/sprint velocity) appear in user T1 and T4 answers but Meta hadn't reached slots that would naturally surface them by the time of termination.

triage_class_per_turn.T4_answer_retry: relevant (added: ['first_run']) — BUT corrupted T5-main prompt context retained from this retry
triage_class_per_turn.T5_answer: silent_zero_F6_surface (covered=[], open=[], conf=0.0, 775ms — F6 backend short-circuit)

comparison_to_M4.delta_verdict: REGRESSION vs M4 — but the regression is NOT in any of A6/A7/A8. It is in the upstream transcript-dedup path triggered by consecutive 500s. With a single flake (like M4) the new stack would likely have matched or exceeded M4 (and probably produced spec.md via A6 lib synth). With two consecutive flakes, the corrupted-prompt pathway took over and the kernel correctly terminated rather than synthesizing on garbage.

root_cause_evidence_pointers.transcript_dedup_bug_evidence: turn5_main_evidence_capsule.json messages[4..7] show assistant-T4-mirror and user-T4-answer appearing TWICE in sequence before the 'Produce your turn-5 output' contract line. This duplication arose during the T4 500->retry sequence: the first failed call appended assistant+user pair to the on-disk transcript without rolling back when the LLM returned ok=false; the retry then re-appended.
root_cause_evidence_pointers.f6_silent_zero_at_T5: session_log.jsonl T5 first attempt: HTTP=200, elapsed=775ms (vs typical ~8000ms LLM round-trip), covered_slots=[], open_slots=[], confidence=0.0. This matches the F6_backend_silent_zero_response.md fingerprint exactly.
root_cause_evidence_pointers.terminate_path_at_T5_retry: session_log.jsonl T5 retry: HTTP=200, elapsed=1046ms, terminated=true, termination_reason='user_input_unparseable_no_spec' — this is the Playback-v2 / kernel double-fail short-circuit firing on the off-topic A5-answers-first_run-drill-question mismatch.
root_cause_evidence_pointers.triage_v3_classifier_itself_NOT_at_fault: turn5_triage_evidence_capsule.json shows triage-v3 system prompt active. T2/T3-retry/T4-retry all credited slots correctly. The classification at T5-retry of 'A5 (robustness) vs question (first_run drill)' as off-topic was technically CORRECT — the bug is that the wrong question was being asked at T5 because of the upstream transcript corruption.

anomalies: Two consecutive SiliconFlow 500 errors hit during the run (T3 first + T3 retry; T4 first). M4 had only one. The on-disk transcript dedup logic does not rollback after a 500, leading to duplicate (assistant,user) pairs in the next turn's Meta prompt.
T5 first attempt returned 775ms with covered=[]/open=[]/conf=0.0 — F6 silent-zero surface fired because the Meta LLM emitted a malformed/empty envelope when given the corrupted-history prompt.

T5 retry then re-prompted Meta from scratch which (per M4 evidence) returns a new question 'sprint速度图具体应该展示什么数据?' — but the test scripted answer A5 was for robustness, so triage-v3 correctly classified as off-topic and the kernel terminated.

CAS audit via CLI (./target/debug/turingos spec audit) entered interactive mode rather than walking the web-driven session — same anomaly as M4. cas_index.jsonl and extracted capsule JSONs in this evidence dir are the authoritative CAS evidence.
A6 library synthesis pipeline never ran (done was never true). Its functional correctness is not exercised by this run; conclusions about A6 cannot be drawn from this single P5 attempt.
A7 triage v3 was active and classified correctly at every successful turn — including correctly flagging the off-topic answer at T5 retry. Triage v3 is NOT the regression source.

A8 playback v2 / double-fail short-circuit appears to be the surface that fired user_input_unparseable_no_spec at T5 retry instead of M4's predicate_done_no_spec_pending_synthesis. This is consistent with A8 design intent (faster terminate on unparseable) but exposes the transcript-dedup bug more aggressively than M4's pipeline did.
recommendations_for_pi4_followup: Re-run P5 codeswitch (and any other Pi4 scenario that hits >=2 500s) until a clean run completes without consecutive upstream flakes — this isolates A6 lib synth correctness from the transcript-dedup bug.

Promote 'transcript persistence does not rollback after LLM ok=false / HTTP 500' to a fix atom (F-series) — this is a NEW class of universality blocker exposed by Pi4's longer answer set + flakier provider window today.

Confirm by inspection of the Meta envelope contract whether T5-first's 775ms empty response should have been treated as parse_fail rather than silent_zero — if so, F6 still has a residual gap that A7/A8 do not cover.
Consider an A9 'transcript rollback on upstream failure' atom before declaring Pi4 acceptance for any code-switch / multi-flake-vulnerable persona.
